codeReview.summary: The import-path migration to flattened modules is largely complete (no remaining `audio.*`/`llm.*` imports), but the server-side ambient note callback was replaced with a no-op, introducing a functional regression in ambient capture persistence.

issues.description: `ServerState` initializes `AmbientService` with `on_note=lambda raw, summary: None`, so `/ambient/start` capture no longer writes notes/topics to the vault. The prior callback behavior was removed in this commit (diff removed `_on_ambient_note`), which breaks ambient-note persistence while the service is running.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill enforces strict RED-first testing, but for repair/refactor tasks like broken-import fixes, workers may need to update existing failing tests directly; the current procedure creates avoidable friction.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoffs/2026-04-10T17-44-48-420Z__fix-broken-imports-tests__791dd9f4-e1df-4aa8-9871-8d74a58b0b9b.json:54-62 records a deviation from step '3. Write failing tests first (RED)' and suggests adjusting skill guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture docs are stale after module flattening and still describe deleted audio/llm modules and outdated component boundaries.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:92 and :94 still reference `llm.extract` and `audio.ambient.AmbientService`; line 26 still states services are plain functions with no classes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Default test command in services registry still skips `tests/test_audio.py` and `tests/test_llm.py`, even though this feature restored those tests and validation now uses full suite.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit d1866b3. Import fixes for deleted audio/llm modules are present, but the feature introduces a blocking regression: server ambient capture no longer persists notes because `AmbientService` uses a no-op callback.
